title: Baccharoides brachylepis
taxonomy: Baccharoides brachylepis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Baccharoides, which is a group of plants native to the Indian subcontinent. The species is further categorized as Baccharoides brachylepis, with its botanical name denoting its classification. Baccharoides brachylepis is closely related to other plants in the same family, sharing similarities in its morphology and growth habits. The exact classification of Baccharoides brachylepis is a subject of ongoing research and study in the field of botany.
uses: Baccharoides brachylepis has been reported to have various medicinal uses, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically verified. The plant is traditionally used in some cultures for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It is said to be used to treat fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The plant's traditional uses are primarily based on anecdotal evidence and have not been extensively studied.

constituents: Baccharoides brachylepis contains sesquiterpene lactones, specifically including compounds like arbusculin, baccharin, and other related sesquiterpene lactones, which are known for their potential bioactive properties.
